Theme Updates

This feature has been removed in Outpost v5.0.

Removed in v5.0 — In v5.0, themes are no longer a concept. The auto-updater only touches the outpost/ engine directory — your site files are never modified by updates.

What changed

In previous versions, Outpost bundled "managed themes" that were updated alongside the CMS engine via a hash-based conflict detection system. In v5.0:

  • There are no managed themes. Your HTML templates, CSS, JavaScript, and images live at the site root and are fully owned by you.
  • The updater only replaces files inside outpost/ — the PHP engine, admin panel, and documentation.
  • Your site files are never read, compared, or modified by the update process.
  • There is no conflict detection because there is nothing to conflict with.

How updates work now

When you update Outpost via Settings → Updates:

  1. The update package is downloaded from GitHub.
  2. Core PHP files in outpost/ are replaced (engine, API, config, etc.).
  3. The outpost/admin/ directory is replaced with the new admin SPA build.
  4. The outpost/docs/ directory is replaced with updated documentation.
  5. Template cache is cleared so recompiled templates pick up engine changes.

Everything outside outpost/ is untouched.

Related docs

  • Auto-Updater — how the update system works in v5.0
  • Site Structure — how site root files work with Outpost
